title: Medicinal Hangover Middle-Eastern Goose Bowl
description: A soothing, herbal dish to cure hangovers, featuring goose fat, beurre-monte, and shojin-friendly ingredients. This hearty bowl is served in a dolsot stone bowl, absorbing the savory flavors.

ingredients.name: goose fat
ingredients.notes: rendered from goose neck or wing meat

instructions: Heat the goose fat in a small saucepan over medium heat.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the beurre-monte and turmeric until smooth.
Cook the quinoa according to package instructions and set aside.
Roast the pumpkin seeds in a 350° oven for 5 minutes, or until lightly toasted.
In the dolsot stone bowl, layer the cooked quinoa, roasted pumpkin seeds, and chopped cilantro.
Drizzle the pomegranate molasses over the quinoa mixture, followed by the beurre-monte-turmeric mixture.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the top and sprinkle with black pepper.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
